T0056.title: Car Loan Cash-Out
T0056.definition: A fraudulent practice of obtaining auto loans or leases using fabricated personal, financial, or employment information without genuine intent to purchase a vehicle. Fraudsters bypass risk controls to secure financing, then dispose of the vehicle to cash out the proceeds, a scheme known as "vehicle financing cash-out."
T0056.description: Also refers to a deceptive transaction method where fictitious car sales are used to defraud lending institutions. For example, a fabricated vehicle purchase contract is used to apply for a loan or lease when no actual car transaction takes place.
